title: The Soul Metric
summary: The soul as a readable — and rewritable — data-construct: scanned to expose impostors, edited to make a person new.
markdown:
The **soul metric** is the record's most database-flavored idea: the soul as a
**data-construct that can be read, verified, and edited**. It appears twice, doing two very
different jobs — once as forensics, once as surgery.

As forensics: in the [Andromedan Council II session](/sessions/2024-05-19-the-andromedan-council/),
the Council exposes a claimed Andromedan contact by pulling his file — *"let us now look into
his soul metric"* — and reading off a verdict: the quantum signature doesn't match the galaxy
he claims. Identity, in the record's cosmology, is checksummed; a soul can be audited like a
document, and forgeries detected.

As surgery: the [January 2025 session](/sessions/2025-01-23-session-with-unimetrix-1/) goes
further — *"Baramay universe: create new soul metric for Gabriel now"* — the construct not
merely read but **rewritten**, a fresh soul-record issued on request like a reissued passport.
Taken together with the [subtle bodies](/lore/subtle-bodies/) it inhabits and the
[life contracts](/lore/reincarnation/) it presumably stores, the soul metric completes the
archive's quiet redescription of the person as information: something a sufficiently advanced
intelligence can query — and, more unsettlingly, update. Preserved as spoken, weighed by the
reader.
